tasks.title: Fix integration test DB table names + run all integration tests green
tasks.description:
The root cause of ALL integration test failures is in `setup.integration.ts` line 33-63. The `cleanDatabase()` function uses PascalCase Prisma model names (e.g. `"Activity"`, `"Comment"`) in the TRUNCATE statement, but every model has `@@map("lowercase_name")` — so the actual PostgreSQL table names are lowercase/snake_case. For example, `Activity` maps to `activities`, `Comment` maps to `comments`, `AIPromptLog` maps to `ai_prompt_logs`, etc.

Fix: Replace all PascalCase names in the `tableNames` array with the actual `@@map` values from the Prisma schema files in `apps/api/prisma/schema/`. The full mapping (read each .prisma file's `@@map`):
- Activity → activities
- Comment → comments
- AIPromptLog → ai_prompt_logs
- AIUsageLog → ai_usage_logs
- CustomFieldValue → custom_field_values
- CustomField → custom_fields
- TaskLabel → task_labels
- Attachment → attachments
- TaskAssignee → task_assignees
- Label → labels
- GitHubPullRequestLink → github_pull_request_links
- GitHubCommitLink → github_commit_links
- GitHubInstallation → github_installations
- Notification → notifications
- NotificationPreference → notification_preferences
- WebhookDelivery → webhook_deliveries
- WebhookEndpoint → webhook_endpoints
- SlackUserMapping → slack_user_mappings
- SlackIntegration → slack_integrations
- AutomationRule → automation_rules
- ProjectMember → project_members
- Report → reports
- SavedFilter → saved_filters
- TaskTemplate → task_templates
- Task → tasks
- Sprint → sprints
- Project → projects
- OrgInvite → org_invites
- User → users
- Org → orgs

After fixing, run `pnpm --filter api test` and ensure all integration tests pass (they were previously all skipped or failing due to this bug). Some integration tests may also need the test database to exist — ensure `setupTestDatabase()` works by verifying `prisma db push` runs against the test DB. If the `tasktoad_test` database doesn't exist, create it. If tests are skipped (conditional on DB connectivity), investigate and unskip them.

Also audit each integration test file to make sure test assertions are sound — the tests haven't actually run in a while so there may be stale assumptions.
tasks.acceptanceCriteria: All integration tests in `apps/api/src/__tests__/` run and pass (no skips due to DB issues). `pnpm --filter api test` exits 0. `pnpm typecheck` passes.

tasks.title: TypeScript strictness audit + expand web test coverage
tasks.description:
Two parts:

**Part 1: TypeScript `any` audit.** Search the entire `apps/api/src/` and `apps/web/src/` for explicit `: any` type annotations (excluding test files and node_modules). The codebase is already well-typed (mostly uses `unknown`), but audit for remaining `any` types. For each one found, replace with a proper type or `unknown` with runtime narrowing. If a type is genuinely needed (e.g. third-party library constraint), add a `// eslint-disable-next-line` comment explaining why.

**Part 2: Web hook tests.** Currently only `useTaskFiltering.test.ts` exists (320 lines). Add tests for the two most critical untested hooks:

1. `useTaskCRUD` — test the core state management: creating a task updates the tasks array, deleting removes it, updating status changes the task in-place. Mock `gql()` from `../api/client`. Test at least: createTask, updateTask (status change), deleteTask, loadComments.

2. `ActivityFeed` component — test rendering: empty state shows 'No activity yet', renders correct number of ActivityItems for given array, displays correct action descriptions (task.created, task.updated with field change, comment.created). Use `@testing-library/react` which is already available.

These don't need exhaustive coverage — focus on happy paths and one error case each. Target ~150 lines per test file.
tasks.acceptanceCriteria: No explicit `: any` types remain (or each is justified with a comment). At least 2 new test files created. `pnpm test` passes. `pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint` pass.

tasks.title: Virtualize ActivityFeed + CommentSection with react-window
tasks.acceptanceCriteria: ActivityFeed virtualizes when > 50 items. CommentSection handles large lists (virtualized or paginated). Dark mode classes added to both. `pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm --filter web build` pass.

tasks.title: Decompose ProjectDetail — extract local state + header/toolbar component
tasks.description:
ProjectDetail.tsx is 1,071 lines — the largest file in the web app. The GPT audit (finding #5) flagged this for decomposition. The data hooks were extracted in Wave 16 but the page component itself still owns too much UI state and renders too many inline sections.

**Part 1: Extract ProjectToolbar component.** The top toolbar section of ProjectDetail (view tabs, search input, filter bar, action buttons like Add Task, template menu, sprint selector) should be extracted into a new `ProjectToolbar.tsx` component. This is the section between the project title and the main content area. It receives callbacks and state as props. Estimate ~150-200 lines extracted.

**Part 2: Move modal visibility state local.** ProjectDetail currently tracks 10+ boolean states for modal visibility (e.g. `showSettings`, `showGitHub`, `showSprintCreate`, `showPRDBreakdown`, `showBugReport`, `showMeetingNotes`, `showCSVImport`, etc.). These should NOT be in the top-level component state since each modal only needs to know if it's open. Move each to a local `useState` within the trigger area, or use a single `activeModal: string | null` state pattern to replace all the booleans. This reduces re-render surface significantly.

**Part 3: Simplify useProjectData interface.** After extracting the toolbar and localizing modal state, some properties can be removed from the `useProjectData` return type. Clean up any now-unused exports from the hook.

Target: reduce ProjectDetail from ~1,071 to ~700 lines. New ProjectToolbar should be ~200 lines.
tasks.acceptanceCriteria: ProjectDetail.tsx is under 800 lines. ProjectToolbar.tsx exists and renders the view tabs + search + filter + action buttons. Modal visibility state is localized. `pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm --filter web build` pass.

tasks.title: Slack self-service user mapping + GitHub webhook dead letter queue
tasks.description:
Two integration completeness items:

**Part 1: Slack `/tasktoad link` self-service command.** In `slackWebhookHandler.ts`, the `handleSlackCommand` function (line 23) processes slash commands. Add a new `link` subcommand that lets users self-map their Slack identity to their TaskToad account:

- `/tasktoad link <email>` — looks up the TaskToad user by email in the org associated with the Slack team. If found, upserts a `SlackUserMapping` record (same as the admin `mapSlackUser` mutation does). Returns ephemeral success message. If no user found with that email, returns helpful error.
- `/tasktoad unlink` — removes the mapping for the calling Slack user. Looks up by `slackUserId` + `slackTeamId`.
- Add `link` and `unlink` to the help text in the `help` subcommand.

The Slack request body includes `user_id` (Slack user ID) and `team_id` (Slack team ID) which are already parsed. Use `prisma` from context.ts import (already imported at line 5).

**Part 2: Webhook dead letter queue.** In `webhookDispatcher.ts`, when a delivery exhausts all 5 retry attempts (line ~70-80 in `attemptDelivery`), it currently sets `status: 'failed'`. Enhance this:

- Add a `deadLetterAt DateTime?` field to `WebhookDelivery` in `webhook.prisma`. Generate migration.
- When a delivery fails after MAX_ATTEMPTS, set `deadLetterAt: new Date()` alongside `status: 'failed'`.
- Add a `deadLetterDeliveries(endpointId)` query in `webhook.ts` typedefs and resolver that returns failed deliveries with `deadLetterAt` set, ordered by most recent, limit 50.
- Update `replayWebhookDelivery` mutation: when replaying a dead-lettered delivery, clear `deadLetterAt` and reset `attemptCount` to 0.

Run `npx prisma generate && pnpm typecheck` passes.
tasks.acceptanceCriteria: `/tasktoad link user@example.com` creates a SlackUserMapping. `/tasktoad unlink` removes it. `deadLetterDeliveries` query returns failed deliveries. Replay clears dead letter state. Run `npx prisma generate && pnpm typecheck` passes. `pnpm lint` passes.

tasks.title: Thread promptLoggingEnabled through AI service layer
tasks.description:
The `promptLoggingEnabled` toggle was added to the Org model in Wave 17, and `callAI()` in `aiClient.ts` already guards prompt log creation with `if (promptLogContext?.promptLoggingEnabled !== false)` (line 161). However, NO callers actually pass `promptLogContext` — the `callAndParse()` helper in `aiService.ts` (line 76-120) only passes `apiKey`, `systemPrompt`, `userPrompt`, `maxTokens`, `feature`, `cacheTTLMs`, and `prefill`.

**Implementation:**

1. **Extend `callAndParse` signature.** Add an optional `promptLogContext?: PromptLogContext` parameter to `callAndParse()`. Pass it through to both `callAI()` calls (line 85 and line 100 retry).

2. **Extend all aiService function signatures.** Each exported function (generateProjectOptions, generateTaskPlan, expandTask, summarizeProject, planSprints, etc. — there are ~25 functions) needs an optional `promptLogContext?: PromptLogContext` parameter as the last argument. Thread it to `callAndParse()`. For the 3 functions that call `callAI` directly (summarizeProject ~line 180, generateCommitMessage ~line 318, enrichPRDescription ~line 382), add `promptLogContext` to their callAI params too.

3. **Pass from resolvers.** In `resolvers/ai.ts`, each resolver has access to `context` (which includes `context.prisma`, `context.user.userId`, `context.user.orgId`). After the existing `requireApiKey(context)` call, construct the `promptLogContext` object and pass it to the aiService function. Load `org.promptLoggingEnabled` — you can add it to the context builder or query it in the resolver. The simplest approach: query `prisma.org.findUnique({ where: { orgId }, select: { promptLoggingEnabled: true } })` once at the top of each resolver (or create a shared helper `buildPromptLogContext(context)` that does this).

Import `PromptLogContext` from `aiClient.ts` in both `aiService.ts` and `resolvers/ai.ts`.
tasks.acceptanceCriteria: All ~25 aiService functions accept optional `promptLogContext`. All AI resolvers pass `promptLogContext` with `promptLoggingEnabled` from the org. When org.promptLoggingEnabled is false, no AIPromptLog records are created. `pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm --filter api build` pass.
